$description:
Relevance bucket definitions for noisy listing endpoints (dead_code, conventions, duplicates). The ?relevance=high|medium|all query parameter / MCP arg is a shorthand the client sends; the server consults this file to decide what to retain. Universal defaults — must be valid for every codebase. Editing this file and restarting the daemon picks up new buckets — no recompile.

dead_code: filter retains entries whose :category is in the listed set. high keeps only :genuine (likely-real dead). medium keeps :genuine + :uncategorized (matches the existing actionable rollup; uncategorized is reserved for future signals).

conventions: filter retains violations whose :severity is in the listed set. Severity strings match the values the analyzer already emits.

duplicates: relevance overrides the supplied/default :threshold to a HIGHER value (never lower). high → 0.95 (very-likely true duplicates). medium → 0.90.

?relevance=all (or absent) means no filter — full unfiltered response, current default.
